講真最主要係兩樣野

1. 用「真誠」呢個主觀字眼任佢判決
本身參選人都要簽聲明架喇
但問題係聲明具法律效力
本身平時d聲明都係架啦 簽嘅就簽
唔會要求真誠地簽
真心又唔得 誠心又唔得 全心全意又唔得
法律野唔應該加呢d主觀字眼

2. 選舉主任作最後定奪冇得上訴
呢個又係香港d權力過大嘅問題
本身個聲明有法律效力
你告佢違反聲明都要交由法庭審
所以更加唔應該比選舉主任話DQ就DQ

例如d過激言論或者強烈爭取民主，都並非代表參選人效忠咗其他國家/政府，參選人仍然係為左香港好，冇得亂咁話人違反聲明

Dear Mr. Fung,

Since 2016, the government has been abusing the “Confirmation Form” to bar candidates with different political views from participating in elections. Regarding this controversy, the Electoral Affairs Commissions is hosting a public consultation on the District Council Election Proposed Guidelines. The Confirmation Form will be introduced into the Guidelines to be enacted by the Electoral Commissioner during District Council Elections, formally censoring and disqualifying candidates, infringing the freedom of expression of the candidates.

According to Article 26 of the HK Basic Law, “Permanent residents of the Hong Kong Special Administrative Region shall have the right to vote and the right to stand for election in accordance with law.”, while political censorship is also infringing Article 25 of the International Covenant on Civil and Political Rights. In Chapter 3 of the Guidelines, Nomination of Candidates, section 3.9(b), it is suggested to formally introduce the Confirmation Form for the Electoral Commissioner to filter candidates. As a Hong Kong citizen, I hereby strongly oppose the aforementioned proposal, the reasons are as follows:

The validity of the one’s declaration is solely determined by the Electoral Commissioner, while a system to appeal and oppose the decision of the Electoral Commissioner is not existent;
The Guidelines suggest that the candidates should “sincerely sign” the Confirmation Form, however there is not an objective definition on being “sincere”, this is in fact literary inquisition, turning Hong Kong into a prison for speech crime.
The Confirmation Form proposed by the government is infringing the rights, protected by the Basic Law and the International Covenant, to participate and voting in election of the Hong Kong people. This is infringing human rights.
The Electoral Commissioner can judge candidates whether their political views can defend the Basic law and remain loyal to the HKSAR base on their past public opinions(even online posts), this is in turn deprivation of political rights.
The government is using the Electoral Commissioner as a tool to disqualify and remove political dissidents from running elections, this is harming the Rule of Law in Hong Kong and adversely impacting the international image and competitiveness of Hong Kong.

Moreover, regarding this public consultation, I have the following opinions:
(Fill in yourself)

Every Hong Kong citizen should have an equal footing in participating elections, I strongly oppose the disqualification of candidates and councillors due to political reasons. I hereby to demand again that:

1) The government to withdraw the introduction of the Confirmation Form from the District Council Election Proposed Guidelines
2) Extend the public consultation period of District Council Election Proposed Guidelines to the 31st of July 2019
3) Host public consultation meetings to listen to public opinions as soon as possible.

I hereby agree the Submission should be treated as a public document.

Yours sincerely,

A Hong Kong citizen
